raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need bash command to delete all hidden files under /workspace. Hidden files start with dot. Recursively? Probably all hidden files and directories. Use find. Command: find /workspace -type f -name ".*" -delete. Also delete hidden directories? maybe -type d -name ".*" -exec rm -rf {} +. Combine. Use -mindepth 1 to avoid root? Provide one line. Let's output: find /workspace -mindepth 1 -name ".*" -exec rm -rf {} +. That deletes files and dirs.
